Caldwell County Stucco Services

Star Stucco of Austin provides honest, affordable stucco repair and installation services to homeowners throughout Caldwell County. From fixing cracked walls on family homes in Lockhart to installing durable new stucco on residences in Luling, we focus on delivering solid work at fair prices that real families can budget for.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Affordable Stucco Solutions for Caldwell County Homes

Caldwell County sits just south of Austin along the I-35 corridor, and the area has seen steady residential growth in recent years as families look for affordable homes within commuting distance of the capital. That growth means more stucco homes, and more stucco homes eventually means more repair work as the Texas climate takes its toll on exterior surfaces.

The black clay soil prevalent across much of Caldwell County is notorious for swelling when wet and shrinking when dry. This constant push and pull puts tremendous stress on foundations and the walls above them. Stucco surfaces crack, separate from trim, and develop moisture problems that worsen over time if they are not addressed properly.

We believe every homeowner deserves quality stucco work regardless of budget. Our approach focuses on honest assessments, practical repair strategies, and transparent pricing. We will never recommend tearing off an entire wall when a targeted repair will solve the problem.

Stucco Services We Offer in Caldwell County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Caldwell County.

Stucco Repair

Many Caldwell County homes were built during periods of rapid development, and some are starting to show wear. We repair cracked, chipped, and water-damaged stucco with an emphasis on fixing problems at the source rather than covering them up with cosmetic patches that fail within a year.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

For new homes and additions in the Lockhart and Luling area, we install stucco systems that account for the challenging soil conditions in Caldwell County. Every installation includes reinforced control joints placed at stress points to minimize cracking as the home settles.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

The expansive black clay soil in Caldwell County is one of the worst offenders for foundation-related cracking in Central Texas. We repair stucco cracks with methods designed to accommodate ongoing soil movement, including elastomeric coatings and properly placed expansion joints.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Flat terrain and poor drainage around many Caldwell County subdivisions allow water to collect near foundations and wick up into stucco walls. We remove water-damaged sections down to sound substrate, correct the drainage issue at the source, and rebuild with waterproof barriers in place.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

A fresh plaster finish can transform the curb appeal of a Caldwell County home without the cost of a full re-side. We offer a range of textures and colors that work well with the residential architecture common in Lockhart, Dale, and surrounding communities.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS is a cost-effective way to add insulation and a clean finish to Caldwell County homes dealing with high summer cooling bills. We install drainage-type EIFS systems and repair older barrier-type installations that may be trapping moisture inside the wall.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Downtown Lockhart businesses, strip-center tenants, and industrial buildings along the highway corridor depend on stucco that looks professional and holds up to daily wear. We work with commercial property owners and managers to deliver durable exterior finishes on practical timelines.

Learn More

Practical repair strategies that address the root cause instead of just masking symptoms

Familiarity with the expansive clay soils in Caldwell County and how they affect stucco walls over time

Efficient crews that minimize disruption to your household and complete work on schedule

Payment flexibility to help families manage larger repair projects without financial strain

Local Expertise

We understand Caldwell County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Caldwell County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Caldwell County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Caldwell County

The combination of reactive clay soil, hot summers, and rapid residential growth creates a predictable set of stucco problems across Caldwell County. Here is what we see most often when inspecting homes in this area.

Cracks

Caldwell County's black clay soil expands dramatically after heavy rains and contracts during dry spells, shifting foundations and opening cracks that run through stucco walls. Homes near Plum Creek and low-lying areas along the San Marcos River are particularly vulnerable to this cycle.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Newer subdivisions near Lockhart and Niederwald sometimes have stucco applied over improperly prepared surfaces or without adequate curing time, leading to soft spots and bubbling as trapped moisture tries to escape through the finish coat.

Water stains

Roof runoff that is not properly channeled away from walls leaves brown and gray stains on stucco, especially around gutter terminations and areas where splash-back from ground-level surfaces throws muddy water onto the lower portions of exterior walls.

Window and door leaks

Production-built homes in Caldwell County subdivisions sometimes have minimal flashing details around windows and doors. As original caulk fails after a few years, water penetrates these openings and causes interior damage that is expensive to repair if not caught early.

Failing patches

Homeowners who attempt stucco repairs with big-box store patching compounds often find that the fix peels away within months. Without proper surface prep and compatible materials, these patches create more problems than they solve.

Separation around trim

The temperature swings between Caldwell County's scorching summers and occasional winter freezes cause different materials to expand and contract at different rates, pulling trim boards away from surrounding stucco and creating gaps that invite water and insects.

Impact damage

Spring hailstorms, lawn equipment strikes, and kids playing in the yard all take their toll on stucco walls. Even small chips expose the metal lath beneath to moisture, which leads to rust staining and accelerated deterioration if not patched promptly.

Faded or stained exterior walls

South-facing walls on Caldwell County homes absorb punishing afternoon sun that gradually bleaches pigmented stucco finishes. Meanwhile, sprinkler overspray and soil splash leave mineral deposits along the base of walls that discolor the surface.

How We Work in Caldwell County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Give us a call or fill out the form on our website to book a free on-site inspection at your Caldwell County home. We serve all of Lockhart, Luling, and the surrounding communities.

2

Review scope

We carefully evaluate your stucco from top to bottom, testing for hidden moisture, checking around all windows and doors, and identifying every area that needs repair or attention. You are welcome to walk the property with us so you can see exactly what we find.

3

Get estimate

We put together a clear, itemized estimate that covers the scope of work, materials, and labor. There are no vague line items or inflated charges. If there are ways to save money without cutting corners, we will tell you about them upfront.

4

Complete work

Our team handles the project start to finish, keeping your yard and landscaping protected throughout the process. We do a final walkthrough with you when everything is complete, and we do not leave until you are satisfied with the results.
